name: gemma4-qa
description: QA over a document corpus. The model is not handed the documents — it must use read-only filesystem tools to find and read the right passage, then answer grounded in the text. Single source of truth for both afchat_lab (Python) and the Aristo app (JS): both load this package and supply only the tool implementations for their runtime.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.description: List the files in a directory. Call it first to see which documents exist. Use "." or the corpus root for the top-level documents folder.

path.description: Directory to list. Use "." for the root documents folder.

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read a document's text. Use the EXACT path shown by list_directory. A file can be long; pass head=N or tail=N to read only the first/last N lines instead of the whole file. Output is capped; if cut it ends with a visible "[TRUNCATED: showed X of Y chars ...]" marker meaning the rest was NOT shown.

path.description: Path to the file, relative to the documents folder, e.g. "manual.md".

head.description: If set, read only the first N lines.

tail.description: If set, read only the last N lines.

tools.name: search_content
tools.description: Search for text INSIDE the documents (a content/grep search). Returns the matching lines with their file and line number. This is the fastest way to locate a fact without reading whole files. Matching is case-insensitive substring, so each term is a short exact word/number/unit — not a whole sentence or a glob like '*length*'. To try several wordings at once, pass a LIST of terms in `pattern` (e.g. ["אורך", "length"]); a line matches if it contains ANY of them. Use pattern='## ' to list every section heading (a table of contents), and pass context=N to also return the N lines AFTER each match — search a section's heading with context=25 to read that section's body.

pattern.description: A search term, or a LIST of terms to match any of them (e.g. ["אורך", "length"]). Each term is a short exact substring (a word, number, or unit). Use '## ' to list section headings.

path.description: Optional file (or list of files) to restrict the search to. Defaults to all documents.

context.description: Number of lines to also return AFTER each match (like grep -A). Use ~20-30 to read a section body after matching its heading. Default 0.
